title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her former patient, Giovanni Thompson.
Giovanni Thompson was a patient at Franciscan Children's with Morquio syndrome and inspired Donnelly with his resilient spirit.
Donnelly, a physical therapist, reflects on Gio's impact, highlighting his humor and perseverance despite his health challenges.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Bruins lose to Devils 4-3 in OT; Jack Hughes logs three assists for New Jersey.
David Pastrnak scores twice for Boston, including a game-tying goal in the third period.
Boston holds the final wild-card spot in the Eastern Conference with 81 points, leading Columbus by two.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, of Tyngsborough, charged with murder after remains found in garage are believed to be missing Lowell woman.
Prosecutors allege Sullivan shot Jill Kloppenburg, missing since January 2025, and buried her under his garage floor.
Investigators found remains during search at Sullivan's home; Office of Medical Examiner identifying remains and cause of death.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman, 45, from Houston, Texas, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Felterman's son, Charles, has Dup15q syndrome, and receives specialized care from Dr. Ronald Thibert at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Felterman honors her son and Dr. Thibert, crediting the doctor's expertise for improving Charles's quality of life.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his wife, Coney Flowers, a leukemia survivor.
Coney Flowers was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia in 2024 and underwent a successful bone-marrow transplant.
Ben is raising funds for Caring for a Cure, an organization that supported them during Coney's treatment.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass General's pediatric cancer team.
Labrie's participation honors children affected by pediatric cancer, each with personal stories connected to the cause.
Donors can honor someone by supporting Labrie’s run, contributing to the I Run For Them initiative.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' will premiere on Netflix on April 13.
Kahan's fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide,' is set for release on April 24.
The documentary explores Kahan's music journey, mental health, and family ties.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall.
Keller's 'Keller At Large' interview show will air Sundays at 8:30 a.m., starting with Gov. Maura Healey.
Keller had been part of WBZ since 2005 and is a 12-time Emmy winner.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider stopping the biannual clock change, with support from Senate President Karen E. Spilka and State Sen. John F. Keenan.
Bill S.2157 proposes adopting permanent Atlantic Standard Time if two or more New England states agree to the change.
83% of Boston.com readers support ending clock changes, citing disruption to routines and health issues; opinions differ on permanent time system.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts teen arrested after leading New Hampshire police on a high-speed chase, crashing into a wall.
The 18-year-old from Stoneham drove 118 mph on Interstate 95 and failed to stop for troopers.
The teen faces multiple felony and misdemeanor charges, including reckless conduct and DUI, held without bail.
